evaluation_inputs.system_description: A large-scale, complex software system currently in Spiral 1 development, intended for significant future enhancement including Net-Ready Key Performance Parameters (NR-KPP). The system utilizes a hybrid architecture combining new N-tier structures with substantial legacy code, documented primarily through hierarchical UML and Requisite Pro artifacts.

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Standard contract documents (CONOPS, SRS, SSDD) were available in paper/electronic document format. However, native electronic design data (Rational Rose, Requisite Pro) was not accessible, making the hierarchical architecture difficult to browse.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: What are the driving architectural constraints, and where are they documented?
How do components communicate and synchronize?
What are the styles of architectural approaches?

hardware_view_description: System must support portability across Linux and Windows NT platforms
Low-level I/O interface layer defined in N-tier architecture to handle platform differences
runtime_constraints: System has a statically set limit for the number of simultaneous connections (links)
Legacy code imposes single-threaded control models on many components

questions: What is the process/thread model of the architecture?
What are the system states and modes?
What variability points are included in the architecture?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: Introduction of a Net-Ready Key Performance Parameter (NR-KPP) for data sharing and enterprise services.
System needs to support a number of links beyond the currently anticipated statically set limit.
Integration of Web Service (WS) based modules across system components.
Replacement of a current component with a known external interface.

business_goals: Achieve Net-Ready Key Performance Parameter (NR-KPP) compliance
Establish an Open Software Architecture design with layered interfaces
Create an integration model for legacy code, new code, and COTS products
technical_constraints: Limited access to electronic CASE tool data
Short timeline and limited resources for assessment (necessitating a hybrid ATAM)

architectural_approaches.description: A layered architecture style used primarily to define the lower-level system infrastructure and I/O handling.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Defines a low-level I/O interface layer to abstract Linux/Windows NT platform differences.

architectural_approaches.description: Extensive use of Unified Modeling Language diagrams (Class, Deployment, Use Case) to document system structure.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Ensures design practices are traceable and understandable across most of the system spiral.

architectural_approaches.description: Reuse of existing legacy code modules wrapped or integrated into the new system structure.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Allows for immediate functionality but constrains architectural evolution.

children.scenario_description: Configure any combination of links up to the static limit
Scale up to a pre-defined number of simultaneous connections
Extending architecture with Web Service (WS) based modules
Handling 200% growth factors in key performance attributes

scenarios.scenario_text: Introduction of a Net-Ready Key Performance Parameter (NR-KPP)
Support for links/connections beyond the statically set limit
Integration of known external interfaces replacing current components
Growth of key performance attributes by 200%

scenario_reference.text: Introduction of a Net-Ready Key Performance Parameter (NR-KPP) to enable net-centric data sharing.

analysis_records.stimulus: Requirement to add enterprise services and data sharing
analysis_records.response_measure: Successful integration without major architectural refactoring
architectural_decisions: Hierarchical design with minimal Web Service opportunities
analysis_records.reasoning: The current architecture provides very limited support for net-centricity. Adding NR-KPP will be difficult because the existing design is hierarchical and relies on legacy structures that do not support these services.

risks.description: Adding NR-KPP may prove difficult and expensive.
risks.rationale: The software architecture has limited current support for net-centric notions.

scenario_reference.text: Scaling the system beyond the pre-defined number of simultaneous connections.

analysis_records.stimulus: Request to add links beyond static limit
architectural_decisions: Reuse of legacy code predating newer link types
analysis_records.reasoning: The architecture is brittle regarding scale. While it works for the pre-defined set, the history of legacy code makes adding support for links beyond this set difficult.

risks.description: Architecture is brittle to supporting scales larger than the pre-defined limit.
risks.rationale: Static limits and legacy code constraints prevent easy expansion of connection handling.

scenario_reference.text: Assessment of process/thread model for concurrent operations.

architectural_decisions: Single threaded model of control from legacy code
analysis_records.reasoning: In too many cases, a single-threaded model of control and data remains from legacy code, which limits performance optimization and concurrency.

risks.rationale: Legacy code enforces single-threaded execution, creating potential bottlenecks.
